title: Cheating
description: Whoever, by deceiving any person, fraudulently or dishonestly induces the person so deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to consent that any person shall retain any property, or intentionally induces the person so deceived to do or omit to do anything which he would not do or omit if he were not so deceived, and which act or omission causes or is likely to cause damage or harm to that person in body, mind, reputation or property, is said to 'cheat'.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year, or fine, or both.

title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
description: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security, or anything which is signed or sealed, and which is capable of being converted into a valuable security, shall be punished.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine.

title: Punishment for criminal breach of trust
description: Whoever commits criminal breach of trust shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both. Criminal breach of trust occurs when a person is entrusted with property, or with dominion over property, and dishonestly misappropriates or converts it to their own use.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years, or fine, or both.

title: Husband or relative of husband of a woman subjecting her to cruelty
description: Whoever, being the husband or the relative of the husband of a woman, subjects such woman to cruelty shall be punished. Cruelty includes wilful conduct likely to drive a woman to suicide or cause grave injury to her life, limb or health, or harassment to coerce her or any relative to meet unlawful demands for property or dowry.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine.

title: Punishment for murder
description: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine. Murder is defined under Section 300 of IPC as culpable homicide committed with intention to cause death, or with intention to cause bodily injury likely to cause death.
punishment: Death or imprisonment for life, and fine.

title: Cheating (BNS Equivalent of IPC 420)
description: Under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023, Section 318 covers cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property. This is the modern replacement for IPC Section 420. Whoever cheats shall be punished with imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both. Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces delivery of property shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine (aggravated cheating).

title: Criminal Breach of Trust (BNS Equivalent of IPC 406)
description: Under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023, Section 316 covers criminal breach of trust. It replaces IPC Section 405/406. A person entrusted with property who dishonestly misappropriates or converts it commits this offence. The section modernizes the language while maintaining the same legal substance.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years, or fine, or both.

title: Cruelty by husband or relatives (BNS Equivalent of IPC 498A)
description: Under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023, Section 85 replaces IPC 498A. It deals with cruelty by husband or relatives of husband towards a married woman. It includes both physical and mental cruelty, harassment for dowry, and conduct likely to cause grave injury or drive the woman to commit suicide.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine.

title: Murder (BNS Equivalent of IPC 302)
description: Under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023, Section 103 replaces IPC Section 302 for murder. Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine. The definition of murder follows from Section 100 of BNS (equivalent to IPC 300).
punishment: Death or imprisonment for life, and fine.

title: Definitions - Consumer Rights
description: Section 2 of the Consumer Protection Act 2019 defines key terms including 'consumer' (person who buys goods or avails services for personal use), 'defect' (fault or imperfection in goods), 'deficiency' (fault in service), 'unfair trade practice', and 'complaint'. A consumer is any person who buys goods for consideration and includes users of such goods.
punishment: N/A - Definitional Section. Forms basis for filing consumer complaints.

title: Manner in which complaint shall be made
description: Section 35 specifies how a consumer complaint is to be filed before the District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission. A complaint can be filed by the consumer, any recognized consumer association, the Central or State Government, or one or more consumers on behalf of others. The complaint must be filed within 2 years of the cause of action.
punishment: N/A - Procedural Section. Enables consumer to seek relief.

title: Jurisdiction of State Commission
description: Section 47 defines the jurisdiction of the State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission. The State Commission has jurisdiction over complaints where the value of goods or services paid as consideration exceeds Rs. 1 crore but does not exceed Rs. 10 crores. It also hears appeals from District Commission orders.
punishment: N/A - Jurisdictional Section. Determines where to file high-value complaints.

title: Functions and Duties of Promoter
description: Section 11 of RERA (Real Estate Regulation and Development Act 2016) outlines the duties of real estate promoters/developers. They must update the RERA website quarterly, not make changes to plans without consent of 2/3rd buyers, maintain separate accounts for each project, refund amounts with interest in case of project failure, and provide all necessary information to buyers.
punishment: Penalty up to 5% of estimated project cost for violations.
triable_by: Real Estate Regulatory Authority / RERA Tribunal

title: Return of Amount and Compensation
description: Section 18 of RERA mandates that if a promoter fails to complete the project or is unable to give possession on time, the allottee can withdraw from the project and the promoter must return the entire amount received with interest at the prescribed rate. Alternatively, the allottee may stay in the project and receive compensation for delay without withdrawing.
punishment: Promoter must refund with interest (typically SBI PLR + 2%). Additional compensation may be awarded.

title: Filing of Complaints with Authority
description: Section 31 of RERA provides the mechanism for filing complaints against promoters, agents, or allottees before the Real Estate Regulatory Authority. Any aggrieved person may file a complaint with the Authority against any promoter or allottee or real estate agent who has violated the provisions of the Act or rules/regulations made thereunder.
punishment: N/A - Procedural Section. Enables filing of RERA complaints.

title: Request for obtaining information
description: Section 6 of the Right to Information Act 2005 specifies the procedure for making a request to obtain information from a public authority. A person who desires to obtain any information under the Act shall make a request in writing or through electronic means in English or Hindi or in the official language of the area to the Public Information Officer (PIO) of the concerned public authority.
punishment: N/A - Procedural Section. Enables citizens to request government information.

title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt
description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both. This section applies to physical assault causing bodily pain, disease, or infirmity.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year, or fine up to Rs. 1000, or both.

title: Punishment for theft
description: Whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both. Theft is defined as dishonestly taking any moveable property out of the possession of any person without that person's consent.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years, or fine, or both.

title: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of peace
description: Whoever intentionally insults, and thereby gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause him to break the public peace, or to commit any other offence, shall be punished. This often applies to cases of verbal abuse, public humiliation, and intentional harassment.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both.

title: Word, gesture or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman
description: Whoever, intending to insult the modesty of any woman, utters any word, makes any sound or gesture, or exhibits any object, intending that such word or sound shall be heard, or that such gesture or object shall be seen, by such woman, or intrudes upon the privacy of such woman, shall be punished. This includes workplace harassment, eve-teasing, and online harassment.
punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 year, or fine, or both.
